week_01: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Dominant 9-1 week, balanced offense and pitching, multiple contributors firing | up
2. BaseballPro77 | Convincing 8-2 win, Mike Trout and Murakami both launched three home runs, clear power threat | up
3. General Hospital | Tied but posted elite team OBP (.414), 23 RBI, and 3 QS — talent is real even without a win | same
4. North Correa | Chris Sale looked dominant with 15 K and 2 QS, offense churned out 23 runs in a 7-3 win | up
5. Benchwarmers | Zach Neto was electric with 8 runs and 2 HR; 7-3 win despite modest pitching | up
6. Athletic Park Dragons | Gutted out a 5-4 win; Gleyber Torres and 4 NSVH were the difference-makers | same
7. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Tied despite 7 HR and solid pitching — Imanaga's 7.00 ERA cost them dearly | down
8. Albuquerque Bombers | Won on pitching alone; Harrison was elite but the offense was paper-thin | same
9. Hiroshima Radiation | Nolan McLean's 15 K was a bright spot but fell 5-4; adding Colin Rea is a smart patch | down
10. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Real offensive upside with Alvarez and Aranda but pitching is a major problem | down
11. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Lost 3-7 but have pitching pieces; Bryan Woo was solid and Soto adds floor | same
12. Buttville Skidmarks | Chase DeLauter's 4 HR is exciting but team ERA of 6.02 is unsustainable | same
13. Canberra Wombats | Dylan Cease looked sharp but Aaron Nola was a disaster; lost 2-8 with zero HR | down
14. Riverside Roughnecks | 1-9 beatdown, 7.91 ERA, zero QS — pitching is in crisis and must be addressed immediately | down
week_02: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Dominant 8–1 win, league-best 40 runs, and consistent top-tier category production across two weeks | up
2. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Historic 9–0 sweep with elite pitching metrics; 75 Ks and 0.84 WHIP are borderline untouchable | up
3. Riverside Roughnecks | Explosive 38-run week and 7–3 win signal a team hitting its stride after a slow Week 1 | up
4. North Correa | Oneil Cruz and Ohtani carried a 7–3 win; pitching depth remains a slight concern but offense is elite | same
5. General Hospital | Posted the best ERA of any winner this season (2.10) and wins despite tough luck; real contender | up
6. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Yordan Alvarez's .600 OBP and team-best 80 IP anchored a 6–4 win; pitching staff is a weapon | same
7. BaseballPro77 | Scraped out a 5–4 win with ugly pitching; two straight wins but underlying stats are volatile | down
8. Hiroshima Radiation | Carlos Correa and Schwarber showed up but the offense collapsed late; 8–1 blowout loss hurts | down
9. Albuquerque Bombers | Kyle Harrison was brilliant but the team still fell 7–3; aggressive waiver activity shows urgency | same
10. Athletic Park Dragons | Aaron Judge and James Wood couldn't overcome poor pitching in a 6–4 loss; floor feels limited | down
11. Buttville Skidmarks | Bryan Reynolds had a nice week but a 7–3 beatdown exposes real pitching and depth concerns | same
12. Benchwarmers | Corbin Carroll is producing but the pitching staff is a liability; barely lost what they should have won easily | same
13. Canberra Wombats | Altuve (.577 OBP) and Henderson were stars but 59.1 IP and weak saves cost them the match | same
14. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | A 9–0 shutout loss with zero stolen bases and a .270 OBP; desperate waiver moves needed immediately | down
week_03: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Three weeks of category dominance, elite pitching ERA of 2.11 this week, and an 8–2 beatdown make them the undisputed No. 1 | same
2. Athletic Park Dragons | James Wood (3 HR, 8 RBI, .636 OBP) and Willson Contreras (3 HR, 11 RBI, .594 OBP) powered a 6–3 win; offense is elite | up
3. BaseballPro77 | Jordan Walker's massive 5-HR week carried a 5–3 win; consistent category producer across three weeks | same
4. Hiroshima Radiation | Bounced back emphatically with an 8–2 rout, 6 QS, and 75 Ks; Bryce Harper's .552 OBP is a weapon | up
5. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Lost 6–3 but posted a freakish 1.65 ERA and 1.04 WHIP; pitching is elite, offense needs to wake up | down
6. General Hospital | Tied despite Manny Machado (3 HR, 11 RBI) and a 70-K pitching week; 5.48 ERA is a concern but talent is undeniable | same
7. North Correa | 11 SB week is elite and Chris Sale delivered 22 Ks, but 4.73 ERA and only 51.1 IP hurt badly in the loss | same
8. Riverside Roughnecks | Won 6–3 on the back of Trevor Story's 9 RBI despite zero QS and a 5.04 ERA; fluky but effective | up
9. Albuquerque Bombers | Acuña, Turang, and Stewart all had strong weeks, but the pitching (5.05 ERA) cost them a winnable matchup | same
10. Buttville Skidmarks | Elly De La Cruz is electric and the ERA (2.25) is elite, but a tie against General Hospital with this pitching staff is a missed opportunity | down
11. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Crushed 8–2 despite decent pitching (3.99 ERA, 75 Ks); bats outside Nimmo and Alvarez are underperforming | down
12. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Shota Imanaga (2.70 ERA, 14 Ks) and a 4.30 team ERA should be winning more; the luck just isn't coming | same
13. Canberra Wombats | Gunnar Henderson (5 HR, 8 RBI) is a stud but the team fell 8–2; pitching gave up too many categories | down
14. Benchwarmers | Won this week but carried by hitters alone; 42.1 IP and a 5.53 ERA is unsustainable and the luck will run out | same
week_04: 1. BaseballPro77 | Historic offensive week with league-record HR and RBI; elite ERA and rotation depth; clear top dog | up
2. General Hospital | 8-category win total this week, dominant pitching (6 QS, 2.68 ERA, 0.96 WHIP), consistent across all categories | up
3. Sugar Land Skeeters | Dropped their matchup but still lead cumulative category wins over four weeks; deep and balanced roster | same
4. Athletic Park Dragons | Aaron Judge continues to destroy, elite pitching staff, 5 QS and solid ERA; winning the right way | up
5. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | 8 QS is a league-high this week, pitching is elite (2.49 ERA, 0.98 WHIP); offense just enough to win | up
6. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | José Ramírez and Yordan Alvarez both exploded; unluckiest team per index despite a W; real upside here | up
7. North Correa | Chris Sale was filthy (0.69 ERA, 0.69 WHIP); lost on pitching ERA meltdown elsewhere but competitive roster | down
8. Hiroshima Radiation | Fantastic pitching (2.81 ERA, 1.09 WHIP, 5 QS) not enough to overcome negative NSVH; dangerous sleeper | same
9. Albuquerque Bombers | Byron Buxton and Sal Stewart carried the week; waiver activity suggests roster instability but scrappy enough to win | same
10. Riverside Roughnecks | Tied when they should have won; Shea Langeliers and Nick Kurtz quietly excellent; pitching ERA needs work | down
11. Canberra Wombats | Tied when they needed a full win; thin offense and limited pitching make ceiling hard to envision | same
12. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Got steamrolled 7–2; historically bad hit total (33 H) and anemic OBP (.257); Ben Rice a bright spot but roster is thin | down
13. Buttville Skidmarks | Lost a winnable matchup; ERA issues and low HR output; Bryan Reynolds' .500 OBP masked a broader offensive slump | same
14. Benchwarmers | Luck can't paper over a 2-category week for long; bottom-tier HR, K, and ERA; Michael King was excellent but can't do it alone | down
